description: You are an Industrial Engineer at a municipal water district. Using the WATER_INFRA schema in Oracle (User: water_eng, Password: Water2024, Host: localhost, Port: 1521, Service: XEPDB1), perform a comprehensive pipe inventory and break history analysis. Calculate annualized break rates by pipe material and installation decade using window functions and group pipes into 5 risk quintiles in a new table FAILURE_RATE_ANALYSIS with columns: material, install_decade, total_miles, total_breaks, breaks_per_mile_year, and risk_quintile. Create a view PIPE_SURVIVAL_VW that calculates cumulative survival probability by material type using analytical window functions. Develop a view ESCALATION_PATTERNS_VW using Oracle's MATCH_RECOGNIZE clause to detect pipe segments with 3 or more breaks with decreasing time intervals between consecutive breaks, outputting pipe_id, pattern_start_date, pattern_end_date, break_count, first_interval_days, and last_interval_days. Generate a table REPLACEMENT_PRIORITY with a composite score for each pipe based on at least 3 factors (such as age, break count, or material risk), storing pipe_id, composite_score, and priority_rank. Create a view BUDGET_PROJECTION_VW using Oracle's MODEL clause to project pipe replacement costs over 10 years (2025-2034) under 3 budget scenarios, calculating miles_replaced and cumulative_spend. Export the top 50 highest-priority pipes from REPLACEMENT_PRIORITY to /home/ga/pipe_replacement_priorities.csv including pipe_id, material, composite_score, and priority_rank.
